title: Present Perfect
description: Bildung, for/since, ever/never, just und Abgrenzung zum Simple Past

exercises.sentence: I ___ finished my homework.
She ___ lived here for years.
He ___ already eaten.
They ___ arrived.
We ___ seen that film.
It ___ stopped raining.
You ___ made a mistake.
My sister ___ called.
The children ___ gone to bed.
Tom and Anna ___ left.
My father ___ worked here for 20 years.
Our teacher ___ explained it.
The dogs ___ eaten their food.
Everybody ___ heard the news.
Nobody ___ answered.
She has ___ (to work) here for ten years.
They have ___ (to finish) the project.
He has ___ (to visit) Paris three times.
I have ___ (to call) her twice today.
We have ___ (to wait) for an hour.
She has ___ (to play) tennis since she was five.
He has ___ (to watch) that film many times.
They have ___ (to live) here since 2010.
I have ___ (to study) English for six years.
She has ___ (to use) this phone for years.
He has ___ (to practice) every day.
We have ___ (to enjoy) every moment.
I have ___ (to learn) a lot this year.
She has ___ (to cook) dinner already.
They have ___ (to clean) the house.

exercises.question: She has ___ (to come) a long way.
He has ___ (to speak) to the manager.
I have ___ (to know) her for years.
She has ___ (to buy) a new car.
I have ___ (to feel) much better lately.
He has ___ (to begin) a new project.
We have ___ (to run) out of time.
